steps.title: Identify the logic of the blank
steps.content: The passage says research in the 2010s suffered from a myopic focus on a few wealthy regions due to limited data. The next sentence says researchers would later ______ this shortcoming after gaining new access to records from Africa and Eastern Europe. The new data gave them a way to fix or improve the flaw—so the blank must mean make better or correct.

steps.title: Eliminate choices that do not mean ‘correct or improve,’ then confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'postulate': To postulate means to assert or assume something as a hypothesis. This would mean researchers proposed the shortcoming existed—not that they fixed it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'ameliorate': To ameliorate means to make something bad less severe or to improve it. New data from Africa and Eastern Europe let researchers correct the myopic regional focus—exactly the right fit. ✓
  • Choice C — 'sanction': To sanction means to officially approve or to penalize. Neither sense fits correcting a research flaw. ✗
  • Choice D — 'rationalize': To rationalize means to justify or explain away. This implies defending the shortcoming, not addressing it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
